text: The oxidative addition of Ni(I) to aryl iodides is a widely accepted if poorly understood step in many catalytic cross-coupling reactions. Here, we present a detailed understanding of the mechanism of this process, obtained by combining the power of electrochemistry with advanced statistical modeling to understand how different aryl iodides react under identical conditions, and how different ligands on the catalytic Ni(I) center influence the rates of oxidative addition. We determined oxidative addition rates to a panel of aryl iodides for four different ligand classes that are relevant to catalysis: Ni(MeBPy), Ni(MePhen), Ni(Terpy), and Ni(BPP) using electrolysis, and then used a data set of over 200 rates to carry out a multivariate linear regression that isolated the electronic and steric effects that determine the rates of oxidative addition and mapped out a global heatmap of rates that can be used to understand reactivity in Ni cross-coupling reactions exemplified with a Ni-catalyzed cross-coupling reaction.
explanation: The text reads as a polished scientific abstract with plausible technical details; while it could be genuine, its generic phrasing and lack of specific experimental data slightly favor AI-generated authorship.
